name: Clarence Sharpe
abstract: Clarence Hardy Sharpe (May 5, 1931, St. Louis – January 28, 1990, New York City) was an American jazz alto saxophonist.

about_quotes.speaker: Arturo O'Farrill
about_quotes.evidence: musicians, older musicians would go there and hang out all day. People like Sonelius Smith, Jimmy Vass, Clarence Sharp — I mean these were places and they would just hang out all day long just jamming and playing... And we would go.
about_quotes.doc_title: Arturo O'Farrill interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: Clarence Sharp, we called [him] C Sharp, he played in Cal’s band as well.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: Cee Sharpe, Clarence Sharpe. I thought he was a magnificent alto player.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
